title: Fragrant Moroccan Oyster Khlea
description: A velvety Moroccan dish that pairs fresh oysters wrapped in a buttery khlea sauce, served hot on a stone plate for a comforting, malty experience.
instructions: Preheat a stone plate over medium heat until hot.
Brush the shucked oysters with a little olive oil and season lightly with salt.
Coat each oyster in breadcrumbs seasoned with cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp of butter and a splash of olive oil in a pan until the butter foams, then add the oiled oysters and brown them on both sides for 3 minutes.
Remove oysters and set aside, reserving the pan drippings.
Add the remaining butter to the pan, whisk in the breadcrumbs, and cook until the crumbs turn golden.
Stir in lemon juice and a pinch of salt, then return the browned oysters to the pan and coat them in the buttery khlea sauce.
Transfer the sauced oysters to the preheated stone plate, garnish with a drizzle of olive oil, and serve immediately.
